name: pcs-tools
description: PCS Tools internal Claude Code plugins for the Kit Builder, MAP pricing, and Jira-task-builder workflows.

plugins.name: pcs-promo-parser
plugins.description: Parse vendor promo decks (Milwaukee, DeWalt, Makita, Bosch, EGO, Flex, GearWrench, Crescent) into PCS Kit Builder Stage 1 outputs. v1.5.0: kit outputs (Promo-List/RSA-Kits) gain a trailing `Page` column and the parser saves each rendered data page to deck_pages/p###.png, so the Jira builder can attach the source deck image and read POS/credit clues (kb ignores the extra column). v1.4.0: robust + selective PDF ingestion (PyMuPDF in-process render when the native rasteriser is missing — no poppler; cover/marketing pages skipped from the vision read for big token savings, no accuracy loss); the Milwaukee SKU pattern now admits sub-brand forms (MX Fuel, letter-infix, bare, Empire, Stiletto, apparel, layout) so legit SKUs stop being held to For-Review; ambiguous split battery slides ask the operator to confirm pairing before holding. v1.3.0: a per-SKU + per-price verification gate independently re-grounds every SKU/price in the deck before writing (deterministic text grep + an adversarial verifier subagent + a vendor-regex check) and HOLDS anything ungroundable out of the Promo-List (For-Review `unverified`) — hallucinated SKUs/prices can't reach NetSuite. v1.2.0: new `Parsed Decks/<Vendor>/<session>/Promo Parsed Output/` folder tree; BMSM, e-rebate, and promo-code promos are parsed into a combined Other-Promotions.csv instead of excluded; a conditional For-Review.xlsx flags low-confidence + missing-data pages; empty outputs aren't written (Parser-Audit.csv is the always-present manifest).

plugins.name: pcs-map-updater
plugins.description: Merge a vendor MAP sheet with a NetSuite ERP export, flag exceptions, and export a review-ready Excel workbook for the monthly MAP update workflow.

plugins.name: pcs-jira-task-builder
plugins.description: Beta. Reads pcs-promo-parser Stage 1 CSVs (Promo Parsed Output/ folder) and creates matching Jira Tasks in the PCS Promotions space. Hard PROM write-protection (literal-phrase gate), defaults to PAT, per-vendor period naming, RSA per-row review, storefront link scaffolding. v0.4.0: titles corrected to PROM convention -- period token = P<N> where N is the deck's quarter number (Q3 -> 2026 P3, not a half-year P2; EGO stays H1/H2, Flex stays Q); the deck promo identifier is KEPT at the end of the title, bracketed ([PCR: P-00208522] / [PCE: 262776]) and omitted on no-ID / consolidated multi-ID tasks; titles generalized to short title-cased phrases with no vendor prefix, no SKUs, no ALL-CAPS / BUY (n)...GET (n) boilerplate; an online-only gate skips any non-online-executable Other-Promotions group (Online Execution No + no online window -- in-store / spend-threshold / volume, e.g. DeWalt PMAPP = IN-STORE ONLY) so it never becomes a Task (RSA stays the tracked exception); field mapping states plainly that PAT is a sandbox whose fields, option labels, and option IDs do NOT match PROM (PAT POS/Online option is labeled 'Option 1'; Promo Deck URL absent on PAT) -- discover fields/options at runtime, skip-and-flag what is missing. v0.3.0: Promo Type by a shared rule (free goods -> Manufacturer Free Goods; NLP -> Manufacturer NLP; coupon/BMSM/buy-X-get-Y -> Manufacturer Coupon; e-rebate -> E-rebate; self-funding left to a coworker; Buy-In is Think-Tank-only, never auto-defaulted); Online Execution = Yes when online dates exist; due = last takedown; POS Redemption = Yes for RSA/credit or a deck-page credit/redemption clue (any vendor); all non-RSA NLPs for a vendor/quarter consolidate into one Task with a sub-task per date group (start-pricing CSV + revert-schedule CSV, revert prices from NetSuite later); deck-page screenshot attached per task; Promo Deck URL set from a Google Drive hub search; Jira attachment token read from a project-folder text file (never chat); auto-HERO removed (no Priority bump / no (HERO) suffix); labels = Vendor + Quarter-Year (e.g. Q3-2026) + promo-type label(s) (Kit-Promo/NLP/Coupon/E-Rebate; +BMSM with Coupon; +RSA); WRITE TO PROM gate unchanged. v0.2.0: also creates Tasks from the parser's Other-Promotions.csv (BMSM / e-rebate / promo-code) with per-group review and BMSM/E-Rebate/Coupon labels; those three are no longer Non-Included reasons; tolerates absent empty files via the Parser-Audit manifest.

plugins.name: pcs-promo-creation
plugins.description: Guided end-to-end promo pipeline: parse a vendor deck -> build NetSuite kit imports -> create Jira promotion tasks, with a Yes/No confirmation before each stage and the hard PROM write-protection at the Jira step. Surfaces a drag-and-drop file-upload artifact at every document step (deck, cheat sheet, NetSuite export). An AI validation pass scans each stage's imports/exports before its gate, auto-correcting safe issues and flagging the rest. v1.16.0: kit stage pulls the master existing-kit list from a team Google Sheet -> build-imports --existing-kits (replace; bundled-snapshot fallback); macOS gets a prebuilt kb-macos binary fetched like kb.exe (floor >= 0.5.24); new final step confirms NetSuite build + offers to refresh the existing-kit list from a fresh NS Promo Kit Support search (append-merge, written back to the Sheet). v1.15.0: requires kb.exe >= 0.5.23 (the choose-N duplicate-kit fix); Step 0's version gate re-fetches it for operators still on 0.5.22 (kb.exe doesn't auto-update). v1.14.0: kit titles tightened — the Page Title cap is reconciled to 90 soft / 95 hard everywhere (removing the stale 80-char self-checks that forced over-truncation), plus descriptor-sourcing precedence (NS Display Name then Page Title then Name, landing on a specific product noun, never a bare 'Tool'), an anti-over-truncation rule, worked examples, and a per-title self-check. v1.13.0: when the NS export shows SKUs not yet built in NetSuite, the kit stage captures a resume kit — the unbuilt-SKU list, an unbuilt DECODE (copy-paste artifact), and a separate Unbuilt-SKU Promo-List of just the affected kits, plus a README — saved to the session folder so a later/separate chat can pick those kits up once the items are built. v1.12.0: the NetSuite kit stage surfaces the Promo Kit Support saved-search link (account 855722, search 16021) in both the Step 3 chat text and the DECODE copy-paste artifact, so the operator clicks straight through to run the search. v1.11.0: token discovery is robust — it finds the GitHub token in a subfolder or oddly-named file and extracts github_pat_/ghp_ by shape instead of assuming a GITHUB_TOKEN= line, and the non-Windows (Cowork Linux sandbox) source install is fixed (token-injected pip URL, drop winget). v1.10.0: Step 0 settles the Kit Builder up front — if `kb.exe` is missing and a `.env` token is present it installs it before parsing (no longer deferred to the kit stage); if no token is found it tells the operator to get the GitHub token `.env` from their admin and offers to continue without kit building (deck parsing + Jira still run; the NetSuite kit stages are skipped). v1.9.0: the Kit Builder ships as a prebuilt headless `kb.exe` — Step 0 checks `kb.exe --version` (>= 0.5.22) and on Windows Claude fetches it from the private Release via the `.env` token (no Python/pip/Git; source pip install stays as the dev/non-Windows fallback). v1.8.0: Stage-1 validation enforces the parser's verification gate — re-matches every emitted SKU to the vendor regex, asserts no held SKU leaked into an output, reconciles SKUs Held, and surfaces verification-held items at Gate 2. v1.7.0: requires kb >= 0.5.21 (collapses duplicate kit members sharing an Item ID); the Step 0 prereq check uses `pip show pcs-kit-builder-lite` (no `kb --version`). v1.6.0: the DECODE formula is shown as a copy-paste artifact (one Copy button per block) in chat. v1.5.0: a run lands in one session folder `Parsed Decks/<Vendor>/<session>/` with Promo Parsed Output / NetSuite Import Files / Images subfolders; the upload widget is recreated on every file (re)prompt; the parser's For-Review workbook is surfaced at the kit gate. v1.4.0: kit images are composed locally via a `kb --images-only` one-liner (macOS + Windows) since Cowork's sandbox can't reach NetSuite's image host. Orchestrates pcs-promo-parser and pcs-jira-task-builder and drives the Kit Builder `kb.exe` CLI binary (>= 0.5.24); Claude writes each kit's Page Title and Detailed Description from the item data using the ported title/description rules; prompts for deck, pricing cheat sheet, and NetSuite Promo Kit Support export uploads when needed.

plugins.name: pcs-facet-enrichment-pipeline
plugins.description: Anglera Faceted Search Backfill -> coverage gap analysis -> HIGH-confidence PDP backfill (real source PDPs, strict v15 dropdown validation, frequency-gated dropdown-addition proposals) -> NetSuite-ready facet import. Operator drops the export, names the brand, and double-clicks a one-click runner; Claude automates gap analysis, scraper config, QA, and NetSuite formatting.
